apply: Apply the concept of domain and range to solve problems related to functions
Use the concept of equality of functions to solve problems
Apply the concept of composition of functions to solve problems related to functions
analyze: Analyze and interpret the graphical representation of functions
Analyze and compare different types of functions and their properties
Analyze and solve problems related to inverse of a function
remember: Recall and identify different types of relations and functions
List and describe various types of functions such as one-one, many-one, onto, into and bijective functions
Identify the domain, range and co-domain of a function
understand: Understand the concept of equality of functions and composition of functions
Appreciate the concept of inverse of a function and its significance
Recognize the difference between a relation and a function

ncert_objectives: Define relation from a set to another set. Explain the difference between a relation and a function
Distinguish between a one-one and many-one function. State the condition for a function to be one-one.
Define the composition of two functions. State the condition for the composition of two functions to be one-one or onto.
Define the inverse of a function and state the condition for the existence of an inverse of a function.
State the condition for a function to be onto.
board_exam_objectives: To test the understanding of the concept of relation and functions
To test the ability to identify and distinguish between different types of functions
To test the ability to apply the concept of domain, range and co-domain to solve problems related to functions
To test the understanding of the concept of equality of functions, composition of functions and inverse of a function
